shortDescription.text: The analyzed content was confirmed to be benign and free of malicious intent or security vulnerabilities.

fullDescription.text: This finding indicates that the content under review, after thorough analysis and reconciliation with external threat intelligence, was determined to be legitimate documentation or code, exhibiting no malicious intent, exploit primitives, or insecure behaviors. It may even promote secure development practices.

message.text: The analyzed content, identified as a SKILL.md documentation file, describes standard development practices for Expo Router and EAS Hosting, including explicit recommendations for secure handling of sensitive environment variables (e.g., using `process.env` and avoiding client-side exposure). External threat intelligence, including official Expo documentation and related articles, strongly confirms the legitimacy of the tools and practices described. No confirmed malicious intent, exploit primitives, or insecure behaviors were identified in the content.
